event: 6-weeks history of left lower quadrant abdominal pain
seemingly located on the left ovary
30-centimeter mass of the left broad ligament
uterus and right adnexa were normal
contrast-enhanced computed tomography scan of the abdomen and pelvis
extending along the left psoas muscle
did not respond to intravenous volume replacement with 2 L of Ringer Lactate
transferred to the operating room for an emergency revision surgery
moderately abundant non-purulent serosanginous peritoneal fluid
no surgical wound that might explain the infection
antibacterial treatment with piperacillin/tazobactam and gentamicin
extubated in the postoperative recovery room
transferred to the intensive care unit
discontinuation on day 3 after revision surgery
microbiological analysis of the peritoneal fluid identified Gardnerella vaginalis
metronidazole was added to the antibacterial treatment
Atopobium vaginae was identified in the peritoneal fluid
antibacterial susceptibility testing of Gardnerella vaginalis demonstrated resistance to metronidazole and ciprofloxacin
antibacterial susceptibility testing of Atopobium vaginae showed susceptibility to all tested antibacterials
piperacillin/tazobactam was active against both bacteria
final diagnosis was an early postoperative peritonitis-induced septic shock caused by Gardnerella vaginalis and Atobopium vaginae
discharged from the intensive care unit
